coupling.note: CacheConfig as Context.Tag in cache.ts forces every ToolCache consumer to also provide CacheConfig. Config should be a factory parameter, not a DI service. fromPlugin error wrapping adds success-path dependency between registry.ts and ToolError classes. registry.ts already imports ToolCache — no new coupling from that direction.

debuggability.note: fromPlugin catchAll swallows ALL errors into generic string messages — typed ToolError/TimeoutError/TransientError/ValidationError are discarded. Stack traces lost. TTL parse failures defect with no span annotation. No structured logging on cache eviction.

convergence.note: Plan was written against stale baseline — tool.ts error classes already exist (lines 41-75). File length estimates are off (tool.ts 319 actual vs 199 claimed). Claim atoms metadata mismatched (3 listed vs 8 claimed). Plan describes work already done.

surface_area.note: Adding stats() method to exported Interface (cache.ts) widens the contract mandatoryly — every implementation now needs it. CacheConfig export from cache.ts leaks config internals. fromPlugin return type implicitly widened to include error-success union.

testability.note: No test strategy for TTL expiry races, concurrent eviction, maxEntrySize enforcement, or edge cases (zero/negative config values). Cache's inflight dedup interacts with TTL in untested ways. Cannot verify in 10-line bun -e script.

error_clarity.note: fromPlugin error wrapper: `[id] Plugin tool error: ${error.message}` — no type tag, no stack, no span attributes on the error path. TTL parse failures defect with no hint about which config key caused it. Cache eviction is silent.

reversibility.note: Changes are mostly additive and isolated to 3 files. cache.ts is new (untracked), tool.ts changes are already applied, registry.ts changes are additive. Reverting cache.ts removes all caching. fromPlugin wrapping can be reverted independently. CacheConfig removal from registry.ts defaultLayer is clean. Score 3 only because plan describes work already done as future work.

objections.finding: Plan claims to ADD ToolError, TimeoutError, TransientError, ValidationError — all four already exist. Plan file-length estimate (199→230) is wrong (actual is 319 lines). Stale baseline will cause duplicate declarations or corrupted diffs on execution.

fromPlugin error wrapping uses catchAll that converts ALL errors (including typed ToolError subtypes) into a success response with generic string message. Completely defeats the ToolError hierarchy this plan establishes. Plugin timeouts, transient failures, and validation errors all become indistinguishable string messages.

CacheConfig Context.Tag inside cache.ts creates mandatory DI coupling: every consumer providing a ToolCache layer must also provide CacheConfig. Config should be a factory parameter, not a DI service.
claim_atoms_total declares 8 atoms but only 3 are listed. 5 missing atoms make boundary verification impossible.

conditions_for_approval: C1: Revise the plan against the current working-tree state (git status). Remove the stale tool.ts error-class section entirely. Correct all file-length estimates.
C2: Replace fromPlugin catchAll with catchTag/catchAllDefect that preserves error types. ToolError subclasses must survive the plugin bridge, not be swallowed.
C3: Remove CacheConfig Context.Tag from cache.ts. Use a parameterized layer factory: `layer(config?: Partial<CacheConfig>)` instead of DI service. This decouples config from provisioning.
C4: Populate all 8 claim atoms explicitly in the claim_atoms array, each as a discrete falsifiable statement. Remove the claim_atoms_total field or derive it from the array.

follow_up_issues.description: Add dedicated test suite for cache eviction, TTL expiry races, maxEntrySize enforcement, and inflight/TTL interaction. Use controlled clock (Effect.Clock.TestClock) for deterministic TTL testing.
Add structured logging to cache eviction events and TTL expiry decisions. Use Effect.logDebug with annotateCurrentSpan capturing cache key, entry age, config values.
Define TTL as DurationInput instead of string to avoid runtime parse failures. If string is required for serialization, add validation at the boundary with a default fallback and warning log.
